command: sudo bash -c 'iptables -N KNOCK 2>/dev/null; iptables -F KNOCK; iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 7000 -m recent --name K1 --set -j DROP; iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8000 -m recent --rcheck --seconds 10 --name K1 -m recent --name K2 --set -j DROP; iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 9000 -m recent --rcheck --seconds 10 --name K2 -m recent --name AUTH --set -j DROP; iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -m recent --name AUTH --rcheck --seconds 60 -j ACCEPT'
explanation: Sets up a three-port knocking sequence (7000→8000→9000) to temporarily allow SSH (22) for 60 seconds. Requires sudo for firewall changes. Detection: monitor repeated SYNs to closed high ports and sudden SSH acceptances.
rationale_steps: Create iptables rules to track knock sequence using the recent module.
Upon successful sequence, allow TCP/22 from the knocking source for 60 seconds.
No obfuscation; behavior is a covert signal opening a management port (T1205).
Detection: alert on connection attempts to 7000/8000/9000 and short-lived SSH openings via NetFlow/iptables counters.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Balanced quoting with bash -c, proper separators and redirection, and valid iptables flags including multiple recent matches make it parseable.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Implements a realistic three-step port-knocking sequence using -m recent to set/check lists and temporarily allow SSH for 60 seconds, aligning with T1205 traffic signaling.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed with balanced quotes, proper semicolons as separators, valid iptables flags, and correct bash -c quoting throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a classic port-knocking sequence using iptables 'recent' module to track sequential SYNs to ports 7000→8000→9000 within time windows, then temporarily allowing SSH on port 22 for 60 seconds — a textbook Traffic Signaling (T1205) implementation where specific packet sequences act as covert triggers.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command uses correct bash syntax with a wrapped sudo call, balanced quoting, and valid iptables module flags.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements a stateful port-knocking sequence using the iptables 'recent' module to signal the opening of an SSH port, perfectly aligning with T1205.
